fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: South Carolina CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to South Carolina - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity south carolina, how to transfer CNA license to south carolina, south carolina CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in South Carolina.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a state-approved Nurse Aide training program in South Carolina. If you have equivalent out-of-state experience, you may qualify for endorsement.

steps.title: Create a Credentia Account
steps.description: Register for an account on the Credentia SC Nurse Aide Registry portal by clicking 'First-time user? Click here to register' on the homepage and following the prompts.

steps.title: Apply for Competency Exam
steps.description: Submit an application through your Credentia SC Nurse Aide Registry account to register for the Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Examination.

steps.title: Schedule and Pass Exam
steps.description: Schedule and successfully pass both the written/oral and clinical skills components of the Nurse Aide Competency Evaluation Examination administered by Credentia.

steps.title: Placement on Registry
steps.description: Upon successful completion of the examination, your name will be automatically added to the South Carolina Nurse Aide Registry.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does South Carolina CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in South Carolina. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in South Carolina.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Carolina while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in SC.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to South Carolina.

hero.title: Become a CNA in South Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for CNA certification in South Carolina, create an account and apply through the Credentia SC Nurse Aide Registry online portal, which administers the registry for the South Carolina Department of Health and Environmental Control (DHEC).

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in South Carolina

official_links.registration_steps: Click 'First-time user? Click here to register' on the Credentia SC Nurse Aide Registry homepage, then follow the prompts to create your account by providing personal details and setting a password.
